notes: Subsequences trigger only on parent campaign completed without reply; parent campaigns are still draft and have no leads.

subsequences.source_parent_campaign_name: Email | Probate | Inherited Property Relief Plan | Texas | 2026-05

variants.subject: still looking at {{property_city}} properties
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Just checking back on {{property_address}}.

I’m still looking at inherited/family properties in {{property_city}} where an as-is path may be easier than repairs, cleanout, showings, or a public listing.

Has anything changed, or should I keep this closed for now?

— {{your_name}}
Reply “remove” and I’ll remove you.

variants.subject: how the as-is review works
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

If it ever becomes relevant, the as-is review is simple.

I look at the property as it sits, ask a few basic questions, and tell you whether an as-is option is worth discussing. You do not have to clean it out, repair it, list it, or have every document perfect before asking.

If that would help with {{property_address}}, let me know.

— {{your_name}}

variants.subject: repairs or cleanout
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

One thing I hear often with inherited properties is that repairs and cleanout become the thing nobody has time to start.

If that ever becomes the issue at {{property_address}}, I can give you a read on whether selling as-is is worth considering before the family puts more time or money into it.

Worth keeping on the radar?

— {{your_name}}

variants.subject: if the family is still deciding
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

If the family is still deciding what to do with {{property_address}}, no problem.

I’m only reaching out because an as-is option can be useful when people want clarity before repairs, cleanout, showings, or listing prep.

Should I check back later, or close my notes?

— {{your_name}}

variants.subject: has anything changed?
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Has anything changed with {{property_address}}?

If keeping it is still the plan, no issue. If selling as-is would be easier than fixing, cleaning, or listing it, I can give you a quick read.

— {{your_name}}

variants.subject: should I keep this closed?
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

I had {{property_address}} marked as not active for now.

Should I keep it closed, or would you want a quick as-is read if the family is considering options again?

— {{your_name}}
Reply “remove” and I’ll remove you.

variants.subject: still buying in {{property_city}}
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Just checking back on {{property_address}}.

Has anything changed, or should I keep this closed for now?

— {{your_name}}
Reply “remove” and I’ll remove you.

variants.subject: how the as-is review works
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

If it ever becomes relevant, my first step is just a practical as-is review.

I look at the property as it sits, ask a few basic questions, and tell you whether an as-is option is worth discussing. You do not need to repair, clean, list, or sort out every detail before asking.

If that would help with {{property_address}}, let me know.

— {{your_name}}

variants.subject: repairs, cleanup, or paperwork
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Sometimes the reason an owner avoids dealing with a property is not one big thing. It is the stack: repairs, cleanup, paperwork, timing, and not wanting to start a long listing process.

If {{property_address}} ever falls into that category, I can give you a simple read on whether selling as-is is worth considering.

Should I check back later?

— {{your_name}}

variants.subject: if listing is not the path
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

If listing {{property_address}} is still the best path, you should do that.

I’m only useful if a simpler as-is path matters more than repairs, showings, time on market, or getting everything perfect first.

Want me to keep this closed, or is it worth a quick look?

— {{your_name}}

variants.subject: has anything changed?
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Has anything changed with {{property_address}}?

If keeping it is still the plan, no issue. If an as-is option would be easier than fixing, cleaning, listing, or sorting through every detail first, I can give you a quick read.

— {{your_name}}

variants.subject: should I keep this closed?
variants.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

I had {{property_address}} marked as not active for now.

Should I keep it closed, or would you want a quick as-is read if the property is back on your radar?

— {{your_name}}
Reply “remove” and I’ll remove you.
